articles.title: B.K. Hariprasad takes jibe at B.L. Santhosh on T.N. politics
Murder, abetment accused can't inherit victim's property: Supreme Court
articles.description: The Supreme Court has ruled that individuals accused of murder or abetting murder cannot inherit property from their victims. This disqualification applies to both wills and intestate succession, even if a trial is ongoing. The court emphasized that no one should profit from their own wrongdoing, citing principles of justice and public policy.

articles.title: UFO files spanning decades are released by Defense Department
articles.description: Cold War reports of mysterious rotating saucers; recent sightings of metallic elliptical objects floating in mid-air. Those and other reports of unidentifiable anomalous phenomena or UAPs — the military's term for UFOs — are described in documents released Friday.

articles.title: Here is Yarbo’s promise to fix the robot mower that ran me over
articles.description:
Yesterday, I told you how a hacker ran me over with a robot lawn mower. We explained how thousands of these bladed Chinese robots, made by Yarbo, could be hijacked with ease - exposing people's GPS coordinates, Wi-Fi passwords, email addresses, and more to any casual hacker who comes along. 
Today, Yarbo has issued a thorough 1,200-word response that you can read in full below. The company is confirming the security researcher's findings, apologizing, and providing a detailed plan to tackle many of its self-created security issues head-on. Yarbo writes that it's already temporarily cut off remote access and is addressing many of its most he …
Read the full story at The Verge.

articles.title: All the latest updates on AI data centers
articles.description:
Massive new data centers are the physical foundation for tech companies’ hopes and dreams for AI. But the rush to expand warehouses full of energy-hungry servers has also kicked up fights across the world over their impact on power grids, utility bills, nearby communities, and the environment. 
From audacious plans to launch data centers into space to the latest legal battles over pollution, The Verge has the biggest news and reporting surrounding data centers.


					
43 percent of Americans blame data centers as a major reason for rising power bills.
			
A political battleground is forming around data centers.
			
Are AI data centers coming to your area?
			
Senators are pushing to find out how much electricity data centers actually use
			
How the spiraling Iran conflict could affect data centers and electricity costs
			
Seven tech giants signed Trump’s pledge to keep electricity costs from spiking around data centers 
			
Trump claims tech companies will sign deals next week to pay for their own power supply
			
Anthropic says it’ll try to keep its data centers from raising electricity costs
			
How an ‘icepocalypse’ raises more questions about Meta’s biggest data center project
			
Microsoft wants to rewire data centers to save space
			
New York is considering two bills to rein in the AI industry
			
Elon Musk is merging SpaceX and xAI to build data centers in space — or so he says
			
It’s a new heyday for gas thanks to data centers
			
Meta is spending millions to convince people that data centers are cool and you like them
			
The winter storm tested power grids straining to accommodate AI data centers
			
OpenAI says its data centers will pay for their own energy and limit water usage
			
ICE hits Meta data center project.
			
Microsoft scrambles to quell fury around its new AI data centers
			
Data center projects are dropping like flies.
			
Alphabet is acquiring a data center company.
			
Communities are rising up against data centers — and winning
			
Billionaires want data centers everywhere, including space
			
AI’s water and electricity use soars in 2025
			
Racks of AI chips are too damn heavy
			
The scramble to launch data centers into space is heating up
			
Data center construction moratorium is gaining steam
			
Data centers in Oregon might be helping to drive an increase in cancer and miscarriages
			
Google is turning on the gas for its data centers
			
Why data centers are building their own power plants
			
Tech companies ‘be on alert,’ NAACP says with new guiding principles for data centers
			
Amazon is investing billions more into its Mississippi data centers.
			
xAI faces accusations its data center is polluting the air.

articles.title: Judge rules DOGE used ChatGPT in a way that was both dumb and illegal
articles.description:
The Department of Government Efficiency's cancellation of over $100 million in grants was unconstitutional, according to a ruling on Thursday. In the 143-page decision, US District Judge Colleen McMahon cites DOGE's process for eliminating grants, which involved using ChatGPT to determine if something is related to diversity, equity, and inclusion (DEI).
The ruling, which stems from a 2025 lawsuit filed by humanities groups, says "it could not be more obvious that DOGE used the mere presence of particular, protected characteristics to disqualify grants from continued funding" from the National Endowment for the Humanities (NEH). Judge McMah …
Read the full story at The Verge.

articles.title: ABC and Disney accuse Trump admin of violating First Amendment rights
articles.description:
ABC is accusing the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) of violating its First Amendment rights by making "major shifts in policy and practice" that the network claims will chill free speech.
The network is asking the FCC to "affirm its long-standing approach to the bona fide news interview exemption" for daytime talk show The View, and the agency's support of "public interest services provided by broadcast stations." ABC says that a series of actions from the FCC "suggests that the Commission is implementing major shifts in policy and practice," and that "requires the action of the full Commission and the oversight of the courts." The  …

articles.title: Someone out-Trumped the Trump phone
articles.description:
These are just five of the 29 versions of the Aurora Lux that Dreame claims it will release. | Screenshot: Dreame	

Where's the Trump phone? We're going to keep talking about it every week. We've reached out, as usual, to ask about the Trump phone's whereabouts. This week, Trump Mobile has been trumped.
It's been another long week without the Trump phone, but it's no longer the only nonexistent phone I have to worry about. Robot vacuum manufacturer Dreame has announced its own set of phones that are unlikely to ever launch, and one of them takes the Trump phone playbook and amps it up to 11.
I was alerted to the existence of Dreame's two new phones shortly after I filed last week's report on the T1 Phone's PTCRB certification, though they "launched" las …
Read the full story at The Verge.

articles.title: Frustrated by Iran, Trump at last seizes enriched uranium – but from Venezuela
articles.description:
US energy department says 13.5kg of uranium taken from reactor in Caracas – a fraction of the 408kg held by Tehran
Donald Trump has succeeded in removing a country’s stash of highly enriched uranium – although that country is not Iran.
On Friday, the US Department of Energy announced that “thanks to President Trump’s decisive leadership” 13.5 kilograms (about 30 pounds) of uranium had been removed from a legacy research reactor in Venezuela.

articles.title: Apple reportedly has a deal to use Intel-made chips again
articles.description:
Apple and Intel reached a "preliminary agreement" for Intel to make chips for Apple hardware, The Wall Street Journal reported Friday. Apple had famously moved on from Intel-powered computers with its highly successful transition to Apple Silicon, but now it appears the two companies will be working closely together again.
Intel appointed Lip-Bu Tan as its new CEO in March 2025, and in August, the US government took a 10 percent stake in the company.  
The WSJ says that it's "still unclear" what Apple products will get Intel chips. Apple and Intel didn't immediately reply to a request for comment from The Verge.
Bloomberg reported this we …
Read the full story at The Verge.

articles.description: The United Arab Emirates has borne the brunt of Iranian attacks throughout the war, hardening the government’s resolve to reassess its list of friends and foes.

articles.title: U.S. economy adds 115,000 jobs in April, unemployment rate at 4.3%
articles.description: The U.S. economy added 115,000 jobs in April, as the unemployment rate came in at 4.3%. NBC News' Allie Canal and Caleb Silver, editor-in-chief at Investopedia, report on the gains in the job market despite the ongoing tensions with Iran.

articles.title: Sebi approves over $1 billion Zepto IPO; 5 other companies also get nod
articles.description: Sebi has approved Zepto’s IPO, paving the way for a potential Rs 12,000 crore listing in 2026. The quick commerce startup aims to strengthen its position against rivals while signaling renewed investor confidence in India’s internet and startup listing market.

articles.title: Trump says ceasefire still intact after burst of violence with Iran
articles.description: The president expressed optimism about reaching a deal despite the attacks. Secretary of State Marco Rubio said the U.S. is expecting a response from Iran on Friday.

articles.title: Bank of Baroda Q4 results: PAT grows 11% YoY to Rs 5,616 crore; NII up 9%
articles.description: Bank of Baroda posted an 11.2% rise in Q4 consolidated profit, driven by higher net interest income, strong loan growth and improved asset quality. Deposits and advances registered double-digit growth, while non-interest income declined and capital adequacy ratio weakened during the quarter.

articles.title: Slow breathing can calm the mind without any need for mindfulness
articles.description: How important is thinking about your breath for calming yourself down? We now know that slow breathing is effective even without conscious involvement

articles.title: Tata Consumer Products Q4 Results: Profit rises 21% YoY to Rs 419 crore, revenue up 18%
articles.description: Tata Consumer Q4 Results: Tata Consumer Products on Friday reported that its consolidated net profit of Rs 419 crore in the fourth quarter. This was higher by 21% from Rs 345 crore posted in the previous year quarter. Revenue from operations increased 18% YoY to Rs 5,434 crore.

articles.title: Bhoy out, bhorsha in: Suvendu Adhikari's message after being picked as Bengal CM
articles.description: Bhoy out bhorsha in Suvendu Adhikaris message after being picked as Bengal CM

articles.title: Ronald McDonald goes viral after singing the 'Star-Spangled Banner' at a minor league baseball game
articles.description: The Charlotte Knights welcomed Ronald McDonald to sing the national anthem at their minor league baseball game, and the video is now going viral.

articles.title: In a 1st, NCRB tracks cases of cruelty against animals: 10,312 arrested in 2024
articles.description: For the first time, the National Crime Records Bureau has tracked animal cruelty cases, registering 9,039 incidents and arresting over 10,000 individuals in 2024. Maharashtra led with the highest number of cases. The data reveals a high conviction rate for concluded cases but significant delays in judicial disposal.

articles.title: AMMK chief Dhinakaran slams TVK for submitting ‘forged’ letter to Governor claiming his party’s backing
articles.description: Mr. Dhinakaran said his party will lodge a police complaint against the incident

articles.title: BJP’s victory in Bengal will plug a big hole in national security, says Amit Shah
articles.description: The victory in West Bengal is significant because after 100 years of ideological continuity, we have a government from every state from Gangotri to Gangasagar, says Shah at meeting of legislators in Kolkata

articles.title: Trump blockade squeezing Iran so hard regime may be dumping oil into Gulf, experts say
articles.description: Experts say Trump's blockade is squeezing Iran so hard the regime may be dumping excess crude into the Persian Gulf as storage capacity runs out.

articles.title: B.K. Hariprasad takes jibe at B.L. Santhosh on T.N. politics
Murder, abetment accused can't inherit victim's property: Supreme Court
articles.description: The Supreme Court has ruled that individuals accused of murder or abetting murder cannot inherit property from their victims. This disqualification applies to both wills and intestate succession, even if a trial is ongoing. The court emphasized that no one should profit from their own wrongdoing, citing principles of justice and public policy.

articles.title: UFO files spanning decades are released by Defense Department
articles.description: Cold War reports of mysterious rotating saucers; recent sightings of metallic elliptical objects floating in mid-air. Those and other reports of unidentifiable anomalous phenomena or UAPs — the military's term for UFOs — are described in documents released Friday.

topStories.title: Delta worker dead after tug vehicle crashes into jet bridge at Orlando International Airport
topStories.description: A Delta Airlines employee was killed when an airport tug vehicle crashed into a jet bridge at Orlando International Airport Thursday night, officials say.

topStories.title: Leftist streamer calls violent revolution 'inevitable' as Democrats explode over Virginia court decision
topStories.description: Democrats explode in fury after Virginia Supreme Court strikes down redistricting map, with leftist streamer Hasan Piker calling violence inevitable.

topStories.title: World News Today Live Updates on May 9, 2026 : US-Iran peace deal soon? Qatari PM Sheikh Mohammed says ‘high probability’, hints at positive progress
topStories.description: World News Today Live Updates on May 9, 2026: Stay informed on global events with our in-depth world news coverage, bringing you the latest developments across politics, economy, and culture. Discover insights into international affairs, breaking news, and the trends shaping our interconnected world, all in one place.

topStories.title: US-Iran peace deal soon? Qatari PM Sheikh Mohammed says ‘high probability’, hints at positive progress
topStories.description: Qatari PM Sheikh Mohammed bin Abdulrahman bin Jassim Al-Thani expressed optimism about a diplomatic solution between the US and Iran despite rising tensions, while warning that the closure of the Strait of Hormuz is harming global interests.

dailyNews.title: All the latest updates on AI data centers
dailyNews.description:
Massive new data centers are the physical foundation for tech companies’ hopes and dreams for AI. But the rush to expand warehouses full of energy-hungry servers has also kicked up fights across the world over their impact on power grids, utility bills, nearby communities, and the environment. 
From audacious plans to launch data centers into space to the latest legal battles over pollution, The Verge has the biggest news and reporting surrounding data centers.


					
43 percent of Americans blame data centers as a major reason for rising power bills.
			
A political battleground is forming around data centers.
			
Are AI data centers coming to your area?
			
Senators are pushing to find out how much electricity data centers actually use
			
How the spiraling Iran conflict could affect data centers and electricity costs
			
Seven tech giants signed Trump’s pledge to keep electricity costs from spiking around data centers 
			
Trump claims tech companies will sign deals next week to pay for their own power supply
			
Anthropic says it’ll try to keep its data centers from raising electricity costs
			
How an ‘icepocalypse’ raises more questions about Meta’s biggest data center project
			
Microsoft wants to rewire data centers to save space
			
New York is considering two bills to rein in the AI industry
			
Elon Musk is merging SpaceX and xAI to build data centers in space — or so he says
			
It’s a new heyday for gas thanks to data centers
			
Meta is spending millions to convince people that data centers are cool and you like them
			
The winter storm tested power grids straining to accommodate AI data centers
			
OpenAI says its data centers will pay for their own energy and limit water usage
			
ICE hits Meta data center project.
			
Microsoft scrambles to quell fury around its new AI data centers
			
Data center projects are dropping like flies.
			
Alphabet is acquiring a data center company.
			
Communities are rising up against data centers — and winning
			
Billionaires want data centers everywhere, including space
			
AI’s water and electricity use soars in 2025
			
Racks of AI chips are too damn heavy
			
The scramble to launch data centers into space is heating up
			
Data center construction moratorium is gaining steam
			
Data centers in Oregon might be helping to drive an increase in cancer and miscarriages
			
Google is turning on the gas for its data centers
			
Why data centers are building their own power plants
			
Tech companies ‘be on alert,’ NAACP says with new guiding principles for data centers
			
Amazon is investing billions more into its Mississippi data centers.
			
xAI faces accusations its data center is polluting the air.

dailyNews.description: Nonfarm payrolls were expected to increase by 55,000 in April, according to the Dow Jones consensus.

dailyNews.description: Prosecutors charge Florida served as central hub in 2021 assassination of Moise, which set off ongoing political crisis.

dailyNews.description: Trump Announces 3-Day Russia-Ukraine Ceasefire Starting Saturday

dailyNews.title: Canvas is back online, but questions — and final exam disruptions — linger
dailyNews.description: Some schools are warning users not to log back into Canvas yet, after a ransomware group claimed credit for a data breach. Half of North America's higher education institutions use the platform.

dailyNews.title: Judge rules DOGE’s cuts to humanities grants were unconstitutional
dailyNews.description: A federal judge said more than $100 million in cuts to NEH grants were discriminatory, ruling in a case that revealed the inner workings of DOGE.

dailyNews.title: Judge rules DOGE used ChatGPT in a way that was both dumb and illegal
dailyNews.description:
The Department of Government Efficiency's cancellation of over $100 million in grants was unconstitutional, according to a ruling on Thursday. In the 143-page decision, US District Judge Colleen McMahon cites DOGE's process for eliminating grants, which involved using ChatGPT to determine if something is related to diversity, equity, and inclusion (DEI).
The ruling, which stems from a 2025 lawsuit filed by humanities groups, says "it could not be more obvious that DOGE used the mere presence of particular, protected characteristics to disqualify grants from continued funding" from the National Endowment for the Humanities (NEH). Judge McMah …
Read the full story at The Verge.

dailyNews.title: Quote of the day: Norman Vincent Peale on mindset, perspective- ‘A positive thinker does not refuse to recognize…’
dailyNews.description: Norman Vincent Peale believed positive thinking helps people overcome adversity and focus on solutions instead of setbacks. His quote reminds us that optimism is not ignoring problems, but choosing resilience, hope and growth even during life’s most difficult moments.

dailyNews.title: ABC and Disney accuse Trump admin of violating First Amendment rights
dailyNews.description:
ABC is accusing the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) of violating its First Amendment rights by making "major shifts in policy and practice" that the network claims will chill free speech.
The network is asking the FCC to "affirm its long-standing approach to the bona fide news interview exemption" for daytime talk show The View, and the agency's support of "public interest services provided by broadcast stations." ABC says that a series of actions from the FCC "suggests that the Commission is implementing major shifts in policy and practice," and that "requires the action of the full Commission and the oversight of the courts." The  …
Read the full story at The Verge.

topStories.title: Someone out-Trumped the Trump phone
topStories.description:
These are just five of the 29 versions of the Aurora Lux that Dreame claims it will release. | Screenshot: Dreame	

Where's the Trump phone? We're going to keep talking about it every week. We've reached out, as usual, to ask about the Trump phone's whereabouts. This week, Trump Mobile has been trumped.
It's been another long week without the Trump phone, but it's no longer the only nonexistent phone I have to worry about. Robot vacuum manufacturer Dreame has announced its own set of phones that are unlikely to ever launch, and one of them takes the Trump phone playbook and amps it up to 11.
I was alerted to the existence of Dreame's two new phones shortly after I filed last week's report on the T1 Phone's PTCRB certification, though they "launched" las …
Read the full story at The Verge.

dailyNews.title: Frustrated by Iran, Trump at last seizes enriched uranium – but from Venezuela
dailyNews.description:
US energy department says 13.5kg of uranium taken from reactor in Caracas – a fraction of the 408kg held by Tehran
Donald Trump has succeeded in removing a country’s stash of highly enriched uranium – although that country is not Iran.
On Friday, the US Department of Energy announced that “thanks to President Trump’s decisive leadership” 13.5 kilograms (about 30 pounds) of uranium had been removed from a legacy research reactor in Venezuela.
 Continue reading...

dailyNews.title: ABC accuses Trump administration of violating free speech rights
dailyNews.description: The Trump administration is violating ABC’s free speech rights with a regulatory action focused on “The View,” the broadcast network argued in an FCC filing.

dailyNews.title: Rubio charms in Rome, but he says Trump’s verbal attacks may continue
dailyNews.description: The secretary of state held warm meetings with Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni and Pope Leo XIV after the president’s repeated criticism of both leaders.

dailyNews.title: ABC Accuses Government of Violating First Amendment
dailyNews.description: The network’s argument, made to the F.C.C., is the most aggressive posture taken yet by a television network toward the Trump administration.

dailyNews.title: WATCH:  UFC champion 'very blessed' to fight at the White House
dailyNews.description: Undefeated lightweight champion Ilia Tuporia says UFC Freedom 250 will be the "biggest event in sports history."

dailyNews.title: Court rejects Virginia redistricting in a blow to Democrats' counter to Trump, GOP
dailyNews.description: Virginia voters approved redistricting that could help Democrats pick up four House seats. Democrats said it was to counter the gains that Trump and the GOP have picked up in Republican-led states.

dailyNews.title: Trump says ceasefire still intact after burst of violence with Iran
dailyNews.description: The president expressed optimism about reaching a deal despite the attacks. Secretary of State Marco Rubio said the U.S. is expecting a response from Iran on Friday.

dailyNews.title: In Trump's crosshairs, Massie rakes in more than $1M this week while fighting for political life
dailyNews.description: Rep. Thomas Massie's campaign raised more than $1 million in a fundraiser so far this week as he faces Trump-backed rival Ed Gallrein in a heated GOP primary.

dailyNews.title: Trump’s Latest Tariff Setback Looms Over China Talks
dailyNews.description: For the president, a major legal defeat could undercut his leverage as he prepares to head to Beijing next week for high-stakes trade negotiations.

dailyNews.title: Trump announces prisoner swap in 3-day Russia-Ukraine ceasefire
dailyNews.description: President Trump said the agreement will swap 1,000 prisoners from each country after Russia and Ukraine announced separate short-term ceasefire plans.

dailyNews.title: What the Trump administration's latest tariff blow means for businesses
dailyNews.description: A trade court's ruling this week against a 10% U.S. tariff is narrow in scope, offering limited relief to importers. Here's what to know.

dailyNews.title: Trump announces Russia-Ukraine 3-day ceasefire
dailyNews.description: President Trump announced a three-day ceasefire between Russia and Ukraine for Victory Day. CBS News' Olivia Rinaldi reports.
